DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DOCUMENTS WERE PRODUCED, AND THIS IS A MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DEPOSITION AND REQUIRES A SEPARATE STATEMENT. THE COURT HAS NO WAY OF DETERMINING WHAT IS ALLEGED TO HAVE BEEN WITHHELD. PRIVILEGES HAVE NOT BEEN WAIVED. HOWEVER, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR FURTHER DEPOSITION WITH RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. COUNSEL MUST MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE APPROPRIATE BREADTH OF THE CATEGORIES OF DOCUMENTS BEING SOUGHT. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH MOVING PAPERS ARE SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF DECLARANT'S PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR INITIAL BURDEN, BOTH FOR THAT REASON AND BECAUSE THEY HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PREVAIL ON ANY CLAIM. FOR EXAMPLE, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THAT HER CHALLENGE TO THE 2007 IS TIMELY BASED ON THE FRAUD EXCEPTION. DEFENDANTS SEPTEMBER STATEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE RULES NECESSARY FOR A PROPER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR # 5797
